summary: Jeff Atwood shares a curated collection of Steve McConnell's IEEE Software magazine editorials from 1998-2002, recommending them as valuable reading for software developers. He particularly highlights 'Cargo Cult Software Engineering' as a standout piece. The post serves as a resource guide to McConnell's lesser-known writing that was buried on his website. Atwood adds a touch of humor by noting he omitted McConnell's Y2K editorial, using it as a reminder that even respected experts can be wrong sometimes.
key_points: Steve McConnell wrote 20 editorials during his tenure as IEEE Software editor (1998-2002)
These articles cover fundamental software engineering topics from community building to project planning
'Cargo Cult Software Engineering' is singled out as particularly recommended reading
Even highly respected software engineering experts like McConnell can make incorrect predictions (Y2K)
money_quotes: I spared you guys the embarrassing chicken little-esque Y2K editorial.
I guess we can all take heart, even a guy as smart as Steve gets it wrong sometimes...
key_insight: Valuable software engineering wisdom often gets buried on personal websites, and even the most respected experts in the field occasionally get things wrong.
